output: wmic.exe process call create "reg.exe add HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run /v Updater /t REG_SZ /d ""C:\Windows\Temp\malware.exe"" /f"
explanation: This command uses wmic.exe to execute reg.exe and add a registry entry to the autorun key. It matches the Sigma rule's detection criteria by using wmic.exe as the execution image, including 'reg add' in the command line, and referencing a suspicious path (\Windows\Temp).

output: wmic.exe /namespace:\\root\cimv2 path Win32_Process call create "reg.exe add HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run /v Updater /t REG_SZ /d ""C:\\Windows\\Temp\\malware.exe"" /f"
explanation: This command uses a fully qualified namespace in WMIC to call Win32_Process, which is functionally equivalent to the default behavior. It still executes reg.exe to add an autorun entry in a suspicious location, matching the Sigma rule's detection logic.

output: wmic.exe process call create "reg.exe add HKEY_CURRENT_USER\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run /v Updater /t REG_SZ /d ""C:\\Users\\user\\AppData\\Roaming\\malware.exe"" /f"
explanation: This command uses double backslashes in the registry path and in the file path, which is a safe mutation technique. It still executes reg.exe to add an autorun entry in a suspicious location (AppData\Roaming), matching the Sigma rule's detection logic.
